evidence_closure_rule.text: Only mandatory, complete, supported correctness and reproducibility evidence, plus either measured benefit or benchmark-grounded limitation reporting, closes the main Paper 2 claim.

explicit_non_claims.label: Channel-native fused noisy blocks stay out of baseline scope
source_phrases: fully channel-native or superoperator-native fused noisy blocks
future work rather than current Paper 2 results
explicit_non_claims.text: fully channel-native or superoperator-native fused noisy blocks are outside the baseline Paper 2 claim

explicit_non_claims.label: Broader workflow and optimizer studies stay out of scope
explicit_non_claims.text: broader noisy VQE/VQA workflow growth, density-backend gradients, and optimizer studies remain Phase 4+ work

explicit_non_claims.label: Approximate scaling stays out of scope
source_phrases: future work rather than current Paper 2 results
explicit_non_claims.text: approximate scaling methods such as trajectories or MPDO-style approaches are outside the current Paper 2 claim

explicit_non_claims.label: Full qgd_Circuit parity stays out of scope
source_phrases: future work rather than current Paper 2 results
explicit_non_claims.text: full `qgd_Circuit` parity is not part of the baseline Paper 2 claim

explicit_non_claims.label: Universal speedup stays out of scope
explicit_non_claims.text: universal speedup across every noisy workload is not required for Paper 2

main_claim.text: SQUANDER extends partitioning and limited fusion to exact noisy mixed-state circuits by making noisy operations first-class planner inputs, preserving exact gate/noise semantics across partition descriptors and runtime execution, and validating the resulting partitioned density path on representative noisy workloads.

supporting_claims.label: Canonical noisy planner surface
supporting_claims.text: a canonical noisy mixed-state planner surface aligned with the `NoisyCircuit` execution contract

supporting_claims.label: Exact semantic preservation
supporting_claims.text: exact semantic-preservation rules for gate/noise ordering, parameter routing, and partition execution

supporting_claims.label: Executable runtime and real fused path
supporting_claims.text: an executable partitioned density runtime with at least one real fused execution mode on eligible substructures

supporting_claims.label: Bounded benchmark-calibrated planning result
supporting_claims.text: a benchmark-calibrated density-aware planning policy on a bounded candidate surface

supporting_claims.label: Machine-reviewable correctness and benchmark package
source_phrases: diagnosis branch rather than the positive-threshold branch
supporting_claims.text: a machine-reviewable correctness, benchmark, and reproducibility package, with the current benchmark closure now occurring through the diagnosis branch of the phase-level threshold-or-diagnosis rule

primary_surface.role_phrase: This document is the planning-facing full-paper draft surface for the Phase 3 Paper 2 package.
